name: Trummy Young
abstract: James "Trummy" Young (January 12, 1912 – September 10, 1984) was an American trombonist in the swing era. He established himself as a star during his 12 years performing with Louis Armstrong in Armstrong's All Stars.

concerts.label: Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Carnegie Pop Concert: Jazz at the Philharmonic
Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
Midnight Performance: Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
Midnight Performance: Woody Herman and His Third Herd/ Louis Armstrong and His All Stars

about_quotes.speaker: Chubby Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: That was with Charlie Barnet’s band... word was passed through to Trummy to get off the bandstand very quietly. And I had one of those big, black plastic bass cases. And it fit him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chubby Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I pulled out a record and put it on, by Trummy Young, and that’s when I first heard Bird.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: Trummy Young, trombone player, I played with him on the jazz festival in New York.
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Darensbourg
about_quotes.evidence: Trummy Young on trombone, Danny Barcelona on drums... That's when old Louis would play... I didn't never drink much, and Trummy drank a little.

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: he was with me in '43, maybe early '44. That was my second time back at the Park Central, and I had a complete mixed band then.

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: We were doing a tribute to Louis Armstrong. We had Peanuts, Trummy Young, Billy Butterfield, Jack Lesberg and Gus Johnson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999
